steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Her work challenges conventional notions of race, gender, history, and ______ she is credited with expanding the horizons of conceptual photographic art." The blank must (1) complete the list ending with a fourth noun after "and," and (2) connect two independent clauses. After the blank, "she is credited with..." is a full independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — memory,: "memory," ends the list but leaves the two independent clauses joined only by a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — memory, and: "memory," closes the list; "and" is the coordinating conjunction that properly joins the second independent clause. This follows the standard pattern for joining two independent clauses: [IC], and [IC]. ✓
  • Choice C — memory and: without a comma before "and," it appears "and" is still part of the list ("race, gender, history, and memory and she is credited..."), which is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — memory: no punctuation or conjunction to connect the second independent clause — creates a run-on. ✗
That matches Choice B — memory, and.
